title: Why Hydrogen Production Modeling Matters
caption: Parameter study for hydrogen-route screening decisions.
description: The route-screening parameter study compares carbon intensity, energy demand, and maturity across common hydrogen routes. The purpose is to demonstrate how NeqSim cases become decision evidence rather than isolated calculations.
discussion: The graph shows that route selection is a multi-objective problem. A low-carbon route may need more electrical or thermal energy, and a mature route may not meet long-term carbon targets. The book uses this pattern repeatedly: model the route, expose the assumptions, and discuss the trade-off in engineering terms.
calculation_basis: Chapter-specific screening calculations for route screening across grey, blue, green, pink, and selected carrier-adjacent H2 concepts, normalized to the KPIs: route selection, boundary definition, and evidence quality.
study_basis: IEA hydrogen route context and the cited process-design references define the interpretation envelope for the normalized indices.
source_labels: IEA Global Hydrogen Review 2023, Chemical engineering design reference

topic_examples.calculation: Include exploratory solar/photo/bio routes as boundary-definition cases with solar-to-hydrogen or biological-yield indices.
topic_examples.sweep: Solar-to-hydrogen efficiency, reactor area, capacity factor, biological yield, and separation load.
topic_examples.kpis: Land or area intensity, H2 output index, maturity index, separation-energy attention.
topic_examples.source_labels: US DOE photoelectrochemical hydrogen overview, US DOE photobiological hydrogen overview, Holladay et al. hydrogen-production technology review
